note: Foundation shell only. Lore, timeline, characters, enemies, weapons, and permanent 3D assets are not approved yet.

landingChapters.name: The Outbreak
landingChapters.copy: Follow the evidence left behind: a checkpoint that stopped answering, a broadcast cut short, and a city still lit by emergency power.

landingChapters.name: Survivors
landingChapters.copy: Introduce a human presence before a biography: a light behind a curtain, a hand on a radio, a choice to keep moving.

landingChapters.name: The Infected
landingChapters.copy: Let danger arrive as behavior before it arrives as a name: a distant movement, a wrong silhouette, a sound that does not belong.

landingChapters.name: Arsenal
landingChapters.copy: Every tool should feel practical, scarce, and consequential. The pressure comes first; the equipment reveal follows.

landingChapters.name: The Mission
landingChapters.copy: A signal gives the player a direction. The objective is to make the next street matter, then make the next decision harder.

gameDirectionCards.title: Every object asks a question.
gameDirectionCards.copy: Ruined streets, abandoned vehicles, empty checkpoints, emergency light, smoke, and traces of evacuation should make the city feel observed rather than explained.

gameDirectionCards.title: Fear arrives before contact.
gameDirectionCards.copy: Distant movement, partial silhouettes, sound before sight, broken posture, and unnatural stillness establish the threat language before a final infected design is approved.

gameDirectionCards.title: Follow the next signal.
gameDirectionCards.copy: The landing experience points toward a survival objective without locking the project into unapproved factions, names, progression systems, or economy values.

gameDirectionCards.title: Build the gate before the roster.
gameDirectionCards.copy: The hero candidate, engine decision, and Android technical prototype remain the next review gates. Temporary media is useful for testing, never canon by accident.
